page: Microsoft Azure DS2_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is configured at 7.0 GB, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 35 MB, an L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and an L1d cache of 32 KB. It supports hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AES cryptography, and FMA. Additionally, the instance includes 14 GB of bundled local storage and operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place the DS2_v2 in the lower tiers for most compute and memory operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks are categorized as weak, with scores of 1246.49 and 2467.38 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 178.15 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds are 22.78 GB/sec and 18.09 GB/sec. Geekbench results reflect a single-core score of 1036.0 and a multi-core score of 1922.0. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing tasks also register in the poor category. However, the instance achieves an average performance tier in LLM text generation using a small 135M model, delivering 94.89 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for the same model remains weak at 302.74 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS2_v2 offers a low-entry hardware profile with dedicated compute resources rather than burstable allocations, making it a predictable option for lightweight workloads. Given the weak to poor performance in database operations and static web serving throughput, this instance is best suited for low-demand general-purpose tasks. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, small-scale development or testing environments, and simple background processes that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded processing.
description: Microsoft Azure DS2_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 2 vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 14 GB of bundled local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark data indicates generally weak to poor performance across compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though it achieves average performance in text generation for small LLM models. Given its dedicated resource allocation and modest hardware profile, the DS2_v2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for predictable, low-demand workloads. It is best suited for lightweight application hosting, basic web serving, and small-scale development or testing environments that do not require intensive multi-threaded processing or high memory throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS2_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 14 GB of local storage, designed for predictable, lightweight workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine with bundled local storage, designed for predictable and lightweight general-purpose computing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 7.0 GB of system memory and 14 GB of bundled local storage
Supports AVX-512, AVX, AES, and FMA hardware instruction sets
Delivers predictable performance via dedicated physical cores on Hyper-V
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic application hosting, and testing
